dependsOn.guidance: Briefs that must be built before this one — usually the tables this model's relationships point at.

instructions.whoFillsThisIn: Developers (Product can sanity-check the information being stored).
instructions.when: Whenever a page needs to store data. One of these per model/table. Copy it into the same solution folder as the page it belongs to and link it from that page.
instructions.how: Answer in plain English where you can. Then hand it to Claude using the bridge in claude-only/1-translate-to-claude.md along with the page it serves.
instructions.note: Solution = the folder this file sits in. Table / object name = this file's name. The database engine is set once for the whole solution in that folder's application-spec.json.

whatThisModelIs.question: What does this model represent, in one line?
whatThisModelIs.answer: A named role that a user is assigned. Each user has exactly one role, but a role can grant several capabilities (e.g. view and edit access to different areas).

fields.guidance: One entry per piece of data. "type" can be plain (text, number, date, true/false, money, list) — Developers can map it to the real database type.

entries.description: The role's display name, e.g. 'Product Edit'.
The capabilities this role grants — e.g. which area (Product, Code) and access level (View, Edit) it allows.
Optional human-readable explanation of what this role is for.

relationships.guidance: How this model connects to others. Leave entries empty if it stands alone.

lookupsAndPerformance.question: Which fields do we search or filter on a lot?
lookupsAndPerformance.guidance: These usually need an index so the page stays fast. One entry per field.

retention.question: How long do we keep this data / when is it deleted?
retention.answer: Kept indefinitely — roles are configuration, not user data. All five default roles are seeded automatically on first run (before any user exists) — Product Edit, Product View, Code View, Code Edit, plus Super Admin (full permissions: manage users, manage roles, and every view/edit permission the other roles have). Seeding the Role rows is automatic; the Super Admin *user* is created separately, by a person, via CoperativeAI/firstRunSetup.md. A Super Admin can add/remove additional roles later, but the Super Admin role itself cannot be deleted.

sensitiveFields.question: Any sensitive fields (personal data, payment info) that need extra protection?

invariants.question: Anything that must always be true?
invariants.example: An order must belong to a customer.
invariants.answer: roleName must be unique. A role cannot be deleted while any UserCredentials row still references it. The seeded 'Super Admin' role can never be deleted, and its permissions can never be reduced — it's the only role guaranteed to always be able to manage users and roles.

changesOverTime.guidance: You don't fill this in at the start. Each time you come back to change this model, add an entry describing what should change. Keep changes small.

exampleEntry.change: Add a lastLogin date field, nullable.
